race.name: Alpe d'HuZes
race.tagline: Twenty-one hairpins. Six ascents. One day. Cycling's greatest charity climb — EUR 100 million raised and counting.

vitals.registration: Registration via alpedhuzes.nl. Free to ride, but participants must raise minimum EUR 1,500 for KWF Dutch Cancer Foundation.
vitals.feed_zones: Support at base (Bourg d'Oisans) and summit (Alpe d'Huez). Water, food, and medical support throughout the day.
vitals.cutoff_time: All ascents must be completed within the event day. Riders aim for 1-6 ascents based on ability and ambition.
route_options: 1 ascent: 13.8 km, 1,071m climbing — achievable for most cyclists
6 ascents: 82.8 km, 6,426m climbing — the full Alpe d'HuZes

climate.description: Early June in the French Alps means warm valley starts in Bourg d'Oisans (20-30C / 68-86F) and noticeably cooler summit conditions at 1,860m (10-18C / 50-64F). Riders experience this temperature differential multiple times per day as they ascend and descend repeatedly. Generally stable early-summer weather, but Alpine thunderstorms can develop in the afternoon. The lower hairpins can be hot and exposed; the upper sections cool and occasionally windy.
challenges: Repeated temperature swings: warm valley to cool summit, 2-6 times per day
Afternoon thunderstorms possible — lightning at 1,860m is a real hazard
Sun exposure on south-facing hairpins in the lower sections
Summit wind chill on repeated descents can cause accumulated cold

terrain.surface: The D211 road up Alpe d'Huez. Well-maintained French departmental road. Smooth tarmac throughout. The 21 hairpins are numbered and named for Tour de France stage winners. Road surface is in excellent condition.
features: 21 hairpin bends — each numbered and named for a Tour de France stage winner
13.8 km at 7.9% average gradient per ascent
Sections exceeding 11%, particularly in the lower third
Descent on the same road between ascents — repeated descending on tired legs requires attention

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 66. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 66.

course_description.character: One climb. Repeated. The 21 hairpins of Alpe d'Huez — 13.8 km at 7.9% average, with sections exceeding 11% — climbed up to six times in a single day. The route is simple to describe and punishing to execute. Each ascent follows the same road: the steep lower hairpins through the forest, the more open middle section, and the final push into the resort village at 1,860m. Each descent follows the same road back to Bourg d'Oisans. The physical experience changes with each repetition — ascent one is exhilarating, ascent three is grim, ascent six is transcendent. The emotional experience depends entirely on why you're climbing.
course_description.signature_challenge: Repetition. One ascent of Alpe d'Huez is a great cycling experience. Six ascents transform it into something else entirely — a test of physical endurance, mental fortitude, and the power of purpose. The mountain doesn't change, but you do.

logistics.airport: Geneva (GVA) — 2 hours. Lyon (LYS) — 2.5 hours. Grenoble (GNB) — 1 hour (limited flights). Most Dutch participants drive or take organized bus transfers from the Netherlands.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Stay in Bourg d'Oisans (valley base) or Alpe d'Huez (summit). Both have limited accommodation that books out well in advance for the event. Many Dutch cycling clubs and tour operators organize group accommodation and bus transfers from the Netherlands. Camping is popular among Dutch participants.
logistics.food: French Alpine cuisine in Bourg d'Oisans and Alpe d'Huez — hearty mountain fare. Feed stations at base and summit provide cycling nutrition. The post-event atmosphere includes shared meals and celebration.

history.origin_story: Founded in 2006 as a cycling charity event combining the physical challenge of climbing Alpe d'Huez with fundraising for cancer research. The event grew from a small group of Dutch cyclists into the Netherlands' largest sporting charity event, raising over EUR 100 million for the KWF Dutch Cancer Foundation. The 'Zes' in 'HuZes' is Dutch for 'six' — reflecting the goal of climbing Alpe d'Huez six times in a single day. The event has become a cultural phenomenon in the Netherlands, with participants treating it as a pilgrimage rather than a sporting challenge.
notable_moments: 2006: Inaugural edition — a small group of Dutch cyclists climb Alpe d'Huez for charity
Event grows to 5,000 riders — becomes the Netherlands' largest sporting charity event
EUR 100 million milestone in cumulative fundraising for cancer research
The event becomes a Dutch cultural phenomenon — participants train for months and fundraise aggressively
history.reputation: Not a race. Not a sportive. A pilgrimage. The most emotionally charged cycling event in the world.

biased_opinion.summary: Alpe d'HuZes is not a cycling event that happens to raise money for charity. It is a charity event that happens to use cycling's most legendary mountain as its vehicle. The distinction matters. When 5,000 riders climb Alpe d'Huez up to six times in a single day — many wearing jerseys bearing the names of people they've lost to cancer — the physical achievement becomes secondary to the emotional one. The 21 hairpins are still the 21 hairpins, the gradients are still punishing, and six ascents produce more vertical than almost any single-day cycling event on Earth. But what elevates Alpe d'HuZes beyond any sportive or gran fondo is the why. EUR 100 million raised. The largest sporting charity event in the Netherlands. This is cycling at its most human.
strengths: Alpe d'Huez: 21 hairpins, each named for a Tour de France stage winner — cycling's most iconic climb
EUR 100 million raised for cancer research — the largest sporting charity event in the Netherlands
Community spirit unlike any competitive event: 5,000 riders united by a cause bigger than cycling
6 ascents = 6,426m of climbing — an extraordinary physical achievement wrapped in emotional purpose
weaknesses: Single climb repeated: no variety of terrain or landscape — you know every hairpin by ascent three
EUR 1,500 fundraising minimum is a significant commitment beyond the physical preparation
Remote Alpine location: 2+ hours from major airports, limited accommodation options
biased_opinion.bottom_line: The hairpins are legendary. The cause is bigger than cycling. If you've ever wanted to suffer for something that matters, this is where you do it.

final_verdict.one_liner: Twenty-one hairpins. Six ascents. EUR 100 million for cancer research. Cycling's most profound charity event.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want to climb cycling's most famous mountain for a cause that transcends sport. Yes if the Dutch charity community and the emotional atmosphere appeal to you as much as the physical challenge. Skip if you want a traditional sportive with varied terrain and competitive structure. Skip if the EUR 1,500 fundraising minimum is a barrier.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want to climb Alpe d'Huez in a traditional sportive format: try La Marmotte (which finishes atop Alpe d'Huez). If you want a charity cycling challenge in the UK: try RideLondon. If you want Alpine climbing with varied terrain: try the Etape du Tour.

videos.title: l'Alpe d'Huez | A Mythical Mountain
videos.description:
I do not earn any amount of money from my videos. Here is my donation link for those who appreciate my videos: https://paypal.me/MemorableCycling

The best moments of the mythical l'Alpe d'Huez.  l'Alpe d'Huez is a mountain pasture in the Central French Western Alps, in the commune of Huez, which is part of the department of Isère in the region of Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes. Alberto Contador holds the record for the fastest ascent of the climb with 37' 30", achieved during the 2010 Critérium du Dauphiné.

Song 1 John Dreamer - Brotherhood
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MejvKklh4KI

Song 2 Zack Hemsey - See what I've become
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2UeA9Nus9Eg

Song 3 John Dreamer- End of My Journey
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=90PRvlhOLSk

videos.title: Alpe d'Huez - Official Chrono Course 2020 (France) - Indoor Cycling Training
videos.description:
Take your indoor cycling to the next level and bike the world at your own speed:
Dive into an immersive indoor cycling experience with our training app ’Open Road’ for PC. The app lets you take control of the speed of our cycling videos and provides a much better indoor cycling experience than with YouTube. It is compatible with all ANT+ cycling devices including smart trainers, power meters, and speed sensors. Athletes with spinning and exercise bikes without ANT+ compatibility can control the speed manually and enjoy riding with all the important cycling data displayed during the training session.  

Learn more about our training app 'Open Road' and sign up for your free ride on our website: http://bikethe.world

About Alpe d'Huez - Official Chrono Course:
The 21 bends up to Alpe d'Huez does not need much introduction. Its nothing less than the most well-known climb in the world.  It has been immortalized by the Tour de France and has become one of the epicenters for cycling history. Numerous 'battles' between some of the best riders through time has taken place on this climb. This climb is so popular that cyclo tourists from all over the world come to ride it. No wonder it is called the 'Hollywood climb'. But it is not an easy ride and it has many sections with double-digit gradients.  A warm-up before the ride is recommended as the climb starts off with double-digit gradients right away.

You can find this ride on Strava:
https://www.strava.com/segments/683473

Facebook:
Do you like our cycling videos? Then learn more about new rides, our training app 'Open Road', and other cycling related activities by give us a ‘like’ or 'follow' on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/biketheworldvideos/

Strava:
Join our global cycling community and get kudos from fellow cyclists that like to bike the world: https://www.strava.com/clubs/biketheworldcyclingclub

Add your music to the ride:
Most cyclists would agree that the ride to Col du Tourmalet is awesome. But when it comes to music opinions are very mixed. This is why we recommend to mute our soundtrack and add your favorite music. Our soundtrack is compiled from the YouTube Audio Library, and it may not be to the liking of everybody. It is quite simple to add it yourself. Mute the tab and open an extra browser tab. Find a site that offers music (Spotify, YouTube, etc…) and pick the music you like and enjoy the ride... The original sound of the ride has been left out. The natural sound of the surroundings is simply drowned by noise from passing cars, vibrations from the road, wind, and the rider's breathing.

Terms of use:
As a courtesy, this video is free to view for private cyclists at home. Any public and/or commercial use needs a license or membership (contact us at http://bikethe.world for more information). It is not allowed to copy our videos in any way. Likewise, it is not allowed to use our videos outside our YouTube channel or our training app 'Open Road' without written permission. Our videos are protected by international copyright laws.

videos.title: Alpe d'Huez - 1 hour 19 seconds - Full Climb - Cycling Motivation
videos.description:
This is the full climb of Alpe d'Huez - the 21 Bends. If you've ever wondered what its like to cycle up the famous Alpe d'Huez, or if you want to cycle Alpe du Zwift against me, then this is your video. The segment of the climb begins at 2 seconds into the video and the climb is completed in 1 hour and 19 seconds. This was also day 17 of my challenge to cycle Alpe d'Huez every day for 30 days!

Click here for a full video of the climb in: 

1 hour 54 seconds
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ft1_yLlA8T8
1 hour 1 min 14 seconds
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0NMIazuA6Ps
1 hour 2 mins 41 seconds
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cRkeR_CcqQA
1 hour 7 minutes
https://youtu.be/ejVsZkPBd-0

On Strava the 21 bends climb segment is called: Alpe d'Huez 'Vieil Alpe'

You can see my ride on Strava here:

https://www.strava.com/activities/3521299456

PayPal Tip Jar: https://www.paypal.com/paypalme/malcolmmoore0301

If you want to support the local Cycle shop here in Alpe d'Huez, or shop any of the kit I'm wearing, click the link below:

https://www.cyclehuez.com/

I also wear SunGod glasses which are really REALLY good and won't break the bank!
https://sungod.to/PuTYQNDX/

If you're in Alpe d'Huez, let me know in the comments down below, I'm always out riding! Thanks for watching - and please hit that like and subscribe button to help me out.

#AlpedHuez #21bends
videos.curation_reason: Full real-time climb experience showing the complete 21 bends course with good view count.

videos.title: Grimpée de l’Alpe 2025 – Racing on Alpe d’Huez
videos.description:
Grimpée de l’Alpe is a classic mountain time trial from Bourg d’Oisans to the top of Alpe d’Huez – 13 km, 21 bends, and over 1,000 meters of elevation. It’s a short but intense test, and a perfect lead-up to La Marmotte Granfondo Alpes.

This video covers my climb – including pacing, what worked (and what didn’t), and the real feeling of tackling Alpe d’Huez hard.

Whether you’re preparing for La Marmotte, planning to climb Alpe d’Huez yourself, or just enjoy the atmosphere of mountain cycling, I hope this video can inspire you and that it gives a real sense of what the day is like.

📺 This is my second video, following my La Marmotte story. There’s more to come from future races and training, so if you’d like to follow along:

✅ LIKE if you enjoy the video
✅ SUBSCRIBE for more cycling content – race experiences, training insights, and classic climbs

💬 Feel free to leave questions or comments – I’m happy to share what I’ve learned along the way.

F O L L O W T H E J O U R N E Y:
https://www.instagram.com/keeaanbaang
https://www.tiktok.com/@keeaanbaang
https://www.strava.com/athletes/3758836

Thanks to:
🙏 My dad – for joining me on the trip
🙏 My wife – for the support and patience
🙏 Giant Store Andreas Lund – for keeping the bike in top shape

#GrimpeeAlpe #AlpeDHuez #CyclingVlog #Granfondo #HillClimb #CyclingMotivation #LaMarmotte #RoadCycling #AmateurCyclist
videos.curation_reason: Recent first-person race experience on Alpe d'Huez with detailed pacing and strategy insights.

key_challenges.name: First Descent
key_challenges.description: 19k descent with 10k left where riders experience severe leg cramping - hamstrings, abductors, and glutes seizing up.

terrain_notes.text: The climbing totals close to 4500 meters with constant ups and downs throughout the course that riders describe as relentless.

race_day_tips.text: During time trial sections, give it full gas and rest between efforts. If you're breathing at the end of the time trial, you didn't push hard enough.

additional_quotes.text: I'm gonna rest and I'm gonna bury myself if I'm breathing at the end of the time trial I didn't push hard enough I need to annihilate myself

rider_intel.search_text: Alpe d'HuZes is a multi-stage event totaling 280 kilometers with 7700+ meters of climbing staged on Alpe d'Huez. The race features three stages including a 123km opening stage, a second stage with 900m elevation, and concludes with a time trial covering approximately 1100 meters of climbing. Riders face relentless ups and downs throughout the course with total climbing approaching 4500 meters. A particularly challenging section occurs on the first major descent - a 19k descent with 10k remaining where severe leg cramping commonly strikes, affecting hamstrings, abductors, and glutes. The time trial sections demand full gas efforts with strategic rest between pushes. Riders describe the overall experience as hellish and deadly, requiring complete physical commitment. The event serves as an intense training camp preparation, pushing participants to their absolute limits across multiple days of racing.
